content.problemStatement:
Chef is participating in a tournament.

He must face NNN opponents.

If he wins against the iii-th opponent, he will receive AiA_iAiтАЛ coins; whereas if he loses, he will receive BiB_iBiтАЛ coins.

It's guaranteed that AiтЙеBiA_i \ge B_iAiтАЛтЙеBiтАЛ.

Chef doesn't want to bring too much attention to himself, so he decides that he will **lose at least once.**

Under this constraint, what's the maximum total number of coins he can receive?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN тАФ the number of opponents Chef will face.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,тАж,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1тАЛ,A2тАЛ,тАж,ANтАЛ.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,тАж,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1тАЛ,B2тАЛ,тАж,BNтАЛ.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the answer: the maximum number of coins Chef can obtain after losing at least once.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: It's optimal for Chef to win the first match, lose the second match, and win the third match.
This way, he gets A1+B2+A3=5+3+8=16A_1 + B_2 + A_3 = 5+3+8 = 16A1тАЛ+B2тАЛ+A3тАЛ=5+3+8=16 coins.
Test case 222: There's only one match, so Chef has no choice but to lose it, receiving 555 coins.
